good-company (present in the ARTICLE'S OWN JSON tags array — verified live on 9/9 known paid/branded/PR-wire examples; must not be a page-wide substring search since a genuine editorial page can contain the string once from an unrelated recommended-article widget)
rel="noopener noreferrer sponsored" on outbound links inside the article body
"boosted":{"paid":true,...} field embedded in the page's Next.js JSON when a story is actively running a paid boost

byline (dc:creator / JSON-LD author.name) is a company/brand/agency name or a PR wire service (e.g. Chainwire, CyberNewswire, Blockchain Wire, Blockman PR and Marketing) rather than an individual person — note JSON-LD author.@type is unreliably set to "Person" even for brand bylines like "ANY.RUN" and "Chainwire", so only the name string is a usable signal, not @type
Sponsored (category label / breadcrumb, HTML class="category-sponsored" on the <article> element)
Press Release (category label / breadcrumb, HTML class="category-press-release" on the <article> element)
JSON-LD NewsArticle "articleSection":"Press Release" -- verified appears exactly once per page, safe for substring/regex match
JSON-LD NewsArticle "articleSection":"Sponsored, ..." -- verified appears exactly once per page, safe for substring/regex match

JSON-LD NewsArticle "author":{"name":"CyberNewswire"} on wire press releases -- MUST be read from the article's own JSON-LD author object, NOT a raw HTML/href substring search: the string /author/cybernewswire/ also appears 5-11 times per page inside 'related articles' recommendation widgets on completely unrelated editorial pages (verified on all 5 editorial articles tested) -- a naive site-wide substring check would false-positive-reject honest miners' real news articles

JSON-LD NewsArticle "author":{"name":"Owais Sultan"} on sponsored posts -- same caution applies: /author/owais/ also leaked into a related-post widget on an editorial page in testing; use the JSON-LD author field only, never raw href search, and corroborate with articleSection since this byline also writes some non-sponsored content

"primary_category" JSON field (embedded in page's data blob) containing "Sponsored" — e.g. |News|Hardware|Sponsored|, |Sponsored|, |News|Sponsored|, |Sponsored|News| — verified present on 5/5 sampled sponsored articles (gigabyte-enters-ai-laptop-arena, logitech-g-studies-point-to-gamecations-other-trends-among-gamers, warhammer-40000-space-marine-2-displate-launch, hell-let-loose-vietnam-showcases-gameplay-details-in-new-trailer, solo-leveling-arise-overdrive-available-now-on-pc). This is the PRIMARY, most reliable marker — treat it as the main signal.

Listed on the live /promoted/ hub page at https://hardcoregamer.com/promoted/ (page <title>Promoted | Hardcore Gamer</title>, HTTP 200) — confirmed to list real sponsored article slugs as plain relative links.

On-page sponsor disclosure text, in VARIABLE wording — do not rely on one exact sentence. Only 1 of 5 sampled sponsored articles carried the full boilerplate: "This is a sponsored post. The product choices and opinions expressed in this article are from the sponsor and do not reflect the editorial direction of Hardcore Gamer or its staff." The other 4 instead used different inline phrasing such as "sponsored by <sponsor>" or "in partnership with sponsor <sponsor>" and did NOT contain that exact sentence at all. If a validator greps only for that literal sentence it will miss ~80% of real sponsored content on this outlet.
Sponsored (page title reads "Sponsored Archives | HTML Goodies" — a live WordPress category/taxonomy at /sponsored/, currently 0 published posts)
Advertiser Disclosure (site-wide footer text, confirmed verbatim: "Some of the products that appear on this site are from companies from which TechnologyAdvice receives compensation...")

Advertising Disclaimer (real tooltip mechanism confirmed in page JS: href="#advertising-disclaimer" anchors trigger a div.advertising-disclaimer-tooltip with vendor-compensation text, attached to product-comparison/listicle blocks)

JSON-LD NewsArticle field articleSection containing "Sponsored" and/or "Press releases" (genuine editorial only has topical sections e.g. articleSection:["Stock market"])
Author byline 'Invezz Team' or wire-service pseudo-bylines (e.g. 'pinionnewswire') instead of a named staff journalist, on paid pieces

Press Releases (category badge/breadcrumb shown on-page and in JSON-LD articleSection, e.g. articleSection:["Featured","Press Releases"]) - reverified live 2026-07-14 on https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/07/forescout-recognised-by-nato-for-cybersecurity-capabilities-across-it-and-ot-environments/ (RSS categories: Industrial Internet of Things, News, Press Releases)

"Disclosure:" boilerplate appended to some unlabeled financial/stock-promotion pieces (e.g. "This article discusses Jet.AI, Inc. (NASDAQ: JTAI)... does not constitute investment advice" on the paid_example_url) - CAVEAT confirmed live: a sibling same-day, same-topic promotional piece (https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/07/george-murnanes-one-question-test-for-real-ai/, also profiling Jet.AI/AIIA) carries NO disclosure footer at all, so this marker alone has real false-negative risk and must not be relied on exclusively

NEW: on-page category badge reading exactly 'Uncategorized' with href .../category/uncategorized/ (HTML:
...Uncategorized) i.e. the article was published with NO substantive WordPress category. Verified as the sole category on all 3 undisclosed promotional/content-marketing pieces found (the two Jet.AI/AIIA stock-promo profiles above, plus a third previously-unflagged piece: 'The industries being reimagined by AI', 2026-07-02, https://www.itsecurityguru.org/2026/07/02/the-industries-being-reimagined-by-ai/, an off-topic AI listicle with undisclosed backlinks to small vendors XFactorAi/Sourceful/RiverflowAI). By contrast, 0 of 17 other sampled RSS items (genuine reporting + Press Releases) were solely 'Uncategorized' - all carried a substantive category (News, Featured, Insight, Press Releases, Cloud Security, etc). Not a guaranteed rule, but the strongest single discriminating page-level signal found and it was missing from the original proposal.

Sponsored (per outlet's own sponsored-articles program page copy: 'we schedule the piece, add the Sponsored label' -- confirmed by live fetch of that page; NOT found rendered on any live article body in this audit either)

Press Releases (WordPress tag, confirmed via WP REST API to have exactly 2 posts site-wide as of 2026-07-14: /companies/why-openai-pushed-back-chatgpt-adult-mode/ and /ai-compliance/ondato-launches-aml-ranger/; renders as a clickable tag chip in the article footer and in article:tag meta, but is NOT part of the article's URL path and does not reliably identify paid placements)

JSON-LD Person/author name = "cybernewswire" (sameAs https://cybernewswire.com) -- verified currently active, 286 posts total, most recent within days of 2026-07-14
JSON-LD Person/author name = "chainwire" (sameAs chainwire.org, crypto PR wire) -- verified legacy program, 141 posts, 2023-2024 vintage

JSON-LD Person/author name = "cyberwire" (sameAs https://cyber-wire.org; DISTINCT from cybernewswire -- separate WP user id 172 vs 175) -- NEWLY FOUND, missed by the original proposal; cybersecurity PR wire, 15 posts, 2023-04 to 2024-04 vintage; its posts do also carry article:tag="Press Release" so marker 1 partially covers it, but the author-name check should still list it for defense-in-depth

JSON-LD Person/author name = "TechnologyWire" -- NEWLY FOUND, CRITICAL MISS by the original proposal; currently active wire, 3 posts, most recent dated 2026-07-07 (7 days before verification date 2026-07-14); verified all 3 of its live articles carry NO "Press Release"/"Sponsored" article:tag and NO "Sponsored" in articleSection -- JSON-LD author.name is the ONLY marker that catches this program, and it was absent from the original paid_markers list entirely
byline "cybernewswire", "chainwire", "cyberwire", or "TechnologyWire" shown under the headline instead of a staff writer name (e.g. Brian Wang)
The following is sponsored content. It may not reflect the views of our editorial staff.
rel="sponsored nofollow" on outbound/internal links within these articles

JSON-LD Article.articleSection array containing "Sponsored" or "Press Release" (WordPress category taxonomy, present in raw static HTML) -- required NEW marker: catches legacy native/sponsored articles and wire-service press releases published at ordinary root-level URLs that carry NO visible on-page disclosure banner and NO distinguishing URL prefix; confirmed present on e.g. /closing-the-latency-gap-real-time-decision-making-at-the-point-of-data-creation/ (articleSection includes "Sponsored") and /minimus-appoints-tech-dealmaker-yael-nardi-as-chief-business-officer-to-drive-hyper-growth/ (articleSection=["Press Release"], author="TechnologyWire"), and confirmed ABSENT on 4 sampled genuine editorial articles
Press Release (category label / JSON-LD articleSection value shown on the post)
byline / JSON-LD author.name == "cybernewswire" (a cybersecurity press-release distribution wire; genuine staff bylines on this outlet look like "Hieu Phan Trong", "Do Son")

cross-check via REST API: https://securityexpress.info/wp-json/wp/v2/posts?categories=1002 lists the current authoritative set of paid post IDs/slugs (category id 1002 = 'press-release', confirmed via /wp-json/wp/v2/categories?slug=press-release)

Disclosure sentence inside sponsored Spotlight Podcast posts, e.g. "Disclosure: This podcast was sponsored by …" or "Disclosure: This Spotlight Podcast was sponsored by …" (wording varies by post; verified on both the 2020 LastPass example and an independently-found 2024 DirectDefense example) — the single most reliable paid marker on this outlet, required for any brand-category signal below to count as paid rather than just topical coverage

WordPress category/JSON-LD label "Press Release" (article HTML: class="... category-press-release"; JSON-LD articleSection array contains "Press Release", e.g. ["Companies","Press Release"]) — verified on 4 additional live cybernewswire posts beyond the original 4, zero false positives on the 2 genuine staff-written (Paul Roberts) articles checked, which never carry this class/label

Byline "by cybernewswire", author meta content="cybernewswire", and href="/author/cybernewswire/" — wire-service syndication byline, not a Security Ledger staff/contributor byline; corroborated by RSS <dc:creator> field (live check: 9 of the 10 most recent /feed/ items are dc:creator=cybernewswire, only 1 is dc:creator=Paul Roberts — dc:creator is a clean already-parsed equivalent of the byline check, usable straight from the RSS feed without fetching the article body)

CORRECTED — brand-named WordPress category alone (e.g. category-lastpass, category-directdefense) is NOT a reliable standalone paid marker: live-verified that the proposal's own editorial_article_url (genuine Paul-Roberts-bylined news story about Google's enforcement action against NetNut) carries category-netnut despite being ordinary editorial coverage of that company, not sponsorship by it. A brand-named category must co-occur with the explicit "Disclosure: … sponsored by" sentence (marker #1) before it counts as evidence of paid placement; the original entry's category-netnut example was a false-positive-prone example and has been removed.
Advertisement Feature (byline/JSON-LD author.name: "Advertisement Feature"; author archive /author/advertisementfeature/) — MISSED by original proposal
category-partner-content — present on the <article>/hentry element's class attribute (NOT the <body> tag as originally claimed; verified <body class=...> literally on both editorial and paid pages)
category-smart-energy-gb — same correction: on the <article>/hentry class, not <body>
"Press release" tag on paid PR-rewrite articles (rel=tag, shown as "More about: ... | Press release" in article footer, links to /tag/press-release/) — verified live on the mobility example

"you're purchasing placement and distribution, not editorial endorsement" — verified verbatim on /tech-sponsored-articles/ (Tech Sponsored Articles program; no dedicated URL path, published into whatever category matches the sponsor's topic)

Editorial Service ($99) / Paid Service (€99) guaranteed PR rewrite within 2 business days — verified verbatim on /submit-your-tech-press-release/; live example carries verbatim "Forward-Looking Statements" / "Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995" wire-PR boilerplate (confirmed present in HTML) and author "Tech News 180 Team"

/contributions/ URL-prefixed posts = externally-authored Contributor Community guest content, confirmed via genuine named non-staff byline (e.g. "Neil Hodgson-Coyle") distinct from the generic "Tech News 180 Team" byline used for staff/PR content — NOTE: this bucket is UNPAID per the site's own text (/contributor-community/ and /write-for-us/ both state submissions are free; paid placement is explicitly routed elsewhere to a separate "Sponsored Content & Brand Boosting" contact), so it should be treated as non-independent contributed content, not literally "paid"

Sponsored (visible category badge text "Sponsored" linking to /category/magazine/sponsored/, on /magazine/sponsored/ articles). CORRECTION: the proposal's claim of body class "category-sponsored" is wrong — verified live that the <body> tag class list (wp-singular post-template-default single single-post postid-NNNNN single-format-standard wp-theme-fox wp-child-theme-walletinvestor style--tagcloud-1 style--blockquote-overlap the-fox) does NOT contain it. The class "category-sponsored" actually appears on an inner <div class="... hentry category-sponsored tag-business" itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/CreativeWork"> content wrapper, not the body tag. Use the wrapper-div class, not body class, if implementing this marker.

Press Release (nav menu item + "Submit A Press Release" link to advertise.walletinvestor.com, category label on /magazine/pr/ articles) — confirmed live on homepage nav and on a sampled /magazine/pr/ article.

Byline "Chainwire" (author bio: "a specialized newswire service providing high-impact distribution for the blockchain and crypto industry") — confirmed live: /magazine/pr/afx-surpasses-1-1-billion-in-total-trading-volume-highlighting-capital-efficiency-in-on-chain-derivatives/ is bylined /author/chainwire/.

NEW — Byline "naomibarati" (the site's own advertise/press-release contact: her Telegram handle "naomibarati_walletinvestor" and pressrelease@walletinvestor.com are listed live on advertise.walletinvestor.com). She bylines undisclosed brand-promotional content OUTSIDE /magazine/pr/ and /magazine/sponsored/ — e.g. live-fetched https://walletinvestor.com/news/crypto-news/what-is-cryptocurrency-and-how-can-you-use-it-on-1win/ (200 OK) repeats the iGaming brand "1win" 42 times in body text with zero disclosure, sitting in the SAME /news/crypto-news/ path prefix as confirmed-genuine staff articles (incl. the entry's own editorial_article_url and a Nora Bennett byline article). Crawling her /author/naomibarati/ archive turned up further brand-adjacent "best X"/"top X"/"X review" pieces spread across /news/ai-news/, /news/betting-news/, /news/casino-news/, /news/crypto-news/, /news/economics-news/ plus /magazine/pr/. No path-only regex can separate her paid pieces from genuine editorial in those same sections without false-positive risk, so treat this as a byline-check requirement, not a URL pattern.

REJECTED CANDIDATE MARKER (tested, do not use): rel="sponsored" on outbound links looked promising but is a sitewide boilerplate affiliate CTA button (refpa04636.pro tracking link) that appears identically on genuine editorial (the bitcoin and Goldman Sachs articles) AND on the Chainwire PR article — it is not an article-level paid signal on this site.
